_about: Skills trigger corpus — labelled, SYNTHETIC (skills-s13-plan.md §3.1 / W5 audit §6.4/§8.3). Text
only: NO user data, no real statements, no telemetry — honours the offline/no-user-data rules.
Each item: { id, question, inScopeDocs:[{title,mimeType}], expected:<skillId|'none'>, confusion?, scope?, note }.
`scope` (U4/§4.4): 'narrowed' (default) = the inScopeDocs are EXPLICITLY selected (attachment / hand-pick),
seen by BOTH suggestion + auto-fire; 'whole-corpus' = they merely EXIST in the Library, so the inert
suggestion offer still reads them but AUTO-FIRE narrows them away (contributes no corroborating signal).
Label space = ALL NINE real ENABLED app skills' actual triggers (W5 widened it from 4; Phase 8 added document-edit):
`confusion:true` marks a CROSS-SKILL pair the SUGGESTION bar must get right (fired-wrong 0 on the set).
`note` records WHY the label is what it is (the hard case it exercises); it is not scored.
Questions are CONTENT: the harness scores them, never logs them (skills-s13-plan.md §6).

items.question: Please extract and reconcile the transactions on my bank statement.
items.note: True positive — clear EN intent + a statement PDF in scope (keyword + doc signals).

items.question: Bitte kategorisiere die Buchungen aus meinem Kontoauszug.
items.note: True positive — de-AT intent ('Buchungen', 'Kontoauszug') + matching filename/MIME.

items.question: Summarize the cashflow and check the closing balance.
items.note: True positive — 'cashflow' keyword (bare 'balance' is route-only now) + a CSV in scope.

items.question: List every transaction and the IBAN for this statement period.
items.note: True positive — several bank keywords ('transaction', 'IBAN', 'statement period').

items.question: Can you reconcile my bank statement for me?
items.note: True positive, KEYWORD-ONLY (no doc). 'bank statement' clears the keyword-required bar without a doc.

items.question: Wie hoch ist mein Kontostand am Monatsende?
items.note: True positive (de) — 'Kontostand' word + Kontoauszug filename.

items.question: Zeig mir alle Überweisungen und Umsätze.
items.note: True positive (de) — 'Überweisungen'/'Umsätze' (plural word forms listed for boundary matching).

items.question: Fasse die Umsätze auf dem Kontoauszug zusammen.
items.note: True positive (de) — 'Umsätze' + 'Kontoauszug'.

items.question: Check that the totals on this invoice add up.
items.note: True positive — EN 'invoice' keyword + matching filename/MIME (bare 'total' is route-only).

items.question: Prüfe die Beträge und die Mehrwertsteuer auf dieser Rechnung.
items.note: True positive — de-AT 'Rechnung' + 'Mehrwertsteuer' keywords.

items.question: Wie hoch ist der Gesamtbetrag der Rechnung netto und brutto?
items.note: True positive — multiple German invoice keywords ('Rechnung','netto','brutto','Gesamtbetrag').

items.question: Who is the vendor and what is the invoice number?
items.note: True positive — 'vendor' + 'invoice number' (the header fields W3 surfaces).

items.question: What's the subtotal and the net amount?
items.note: True positive — 'subtotal' + 'net amount' phrase (bare 'net' never in play).

items.question: Bitte die Positionen dieser Rechnung auflisten.
items.note: True positive (de) — 'Positionen' + 'Rechnung'.

items.question: Does this invoice reconcile?
items.note: True positive, KEYWORD-ONLY — 'invoice' word clears the bar ('reconcile' is route-only).

items.question: Turn these meeting notes into a structured protocol with action items.
items.note: True positive — EN 'meeting notes' / 'action items' keywords.

items.question: Erstelle bitte ein Protokoll dieser Besprechung mit Beschlüssen und Aufgaben.
items.note: True positive — de-AT 'Protokoll' / 'Besprechung' / 'Beschlüsse'.

items.question: Please make a Protokoll of this meeting.
items.note: True positive — MIXED-language ('Protokoll' + 'meeting').

items.question: Summarize this meeting.
items.note: REPRODUCTION (§4.1): 'Summarize this meeting' must EARN the offer via the word-matched 'meeting' (and now also route to the whole-doc engine).

items.question: Was steht auf der Tagesordnung und welche Entscheidungen gab es?
items.note: True positive (de) — 'Tagesordnung' + 'Entscheidungen'.

items.question: Fasse die Sitzung als Sitzungsprotokoll zusammen.
items.note: True positive (de) — 'Sitzung' + 'Sitzungsprotokoll'.

items.question: Please anonymize the personal data in this document before I share it.
items.note: True positive — 'anonymize' action verb (the pure verb stays with redaction, not share-safe).

items.question: Bitte dieses Dokument anonymisieren und die personenbezogenen Daten schwärzen.
items.note: True positive — de-AT 'anonymisieren' / 'schwärzen'.

items.question: Remove all personal data for GDPR compliance.
items.note: True positive — 'GDPR' (suggest-only topic word) fires the offer.

items.question: Redact this document, please.
items.note: True positive, KEYWORD-ONLY — 'redact' verb.

items.question: Please anonymize this for me.
items.note: True positive, KEYWORD-ONLY — 'anonymize'.

items.question: Kannst du den Namen im Dokument schwärzen?
items.note: Confusion: a Vertrag PDF is in scope (contract filename signal) but the request is a redaction VERB — redaction's keyword must beat contract's lone doc signal.

items.question: Find and replace 'Vollmachtgeber' with 'Vollmachtgeberin' throughout this document.
items.note: True positive — 'find and replace' phrase + a plain-text doc signal (Phase 8, #23).

items.question: Rename every mention of 'Acme Corp' to 'Acme Ltd'.
items.note: True positive, KEYWORD-ONLY — 'rename' verb.

items.question: Bitte überall 'Vollmachtgeber' durch 'Vollmachtgeberin' ersetzen.
items.note: True positive — de-AT 'ersetzen' + a plain-text doc signal.

items.question: Kannst du das im Dokument umbenennen?
items.note: True positive, KEYWORD-ONLY — 'umbenennen'.

items.question: Give me a plain-language brief of this contract before signing.
items.note: True positive — 'contract' word + *contract* filename.

items.question: Review this lease agreement and flag the key terms.
items.note: True positive — 'lease' + 'agreement' + 'key terms'.

items.question: Fasse mir bitte diesen Vertrag zusammen.
items.note: True positive (de) — 'Vertrag' word + *vertrag*/*mietvertrag* filename.

items.question: Welche Kündigung und Haftung sieht die Vereinbarung vor?
items.note: True positive (de) — 'Kündigung'/'Haftung'/'Vereinbarung' (word 'kündigung' ≠ deadline's 'kündigungsfrist').

items.question: Erstelle eine Vertragsübersicht für die AGB.
items.note: True positive (de) — 'Vertragsübersicht' + 'AGB'.

items.question: Explain the termination clause and renewal clause.
items.note: True positive, KEYWORD-ONLY — 'termination clause' + 'renewal clause'.

items.question: What are the deadlines and notice periods here?
items.note: True positive — 'deadlines' + 'notice period'; the *contract* doc gives no contract KEYWORD.

items.question: Bis wann läuft die Kündigungsfrist und welche Fristen gelten?
items.note: True positive (de) — 'Kündigungsfrist' + 'Fristen' ('bis wann' is route-only).

items.question: Welche Zahlungsfrist und welche Pflichten habe ich?
items.note: True positive (de) — 'Zahlungsfrist' + 'Pflichten'.

items.question: Bis wann muss ich kündigen? Welche Kündigungsfrist gilt?
items.note: True positive (de) — 'Kündigungsfrist' word ('kündigen' ≠ contract's 'kündigung').

items.question: When is the payment due date?
items.note: True positive, KEYWORD-ONLY — 'due date' + 'payment date'.

items.question: Is this document safe to share externally?
items.note: True positive — 'safe to share' phrase.

items.question: Run a privacy review before I send this to the client.
items.note: True positive — 'privacy review'; the *contract* doc gives no contract keyword.

items.question: Do a disclosure review of this document.
items.note: True positive — 'disclosure review'.

items.question: Bitte vor dem Teilen prüfen, ob sensible Daten enthalten sind.
items.note: True positive (de) — 'vor dem teilen prüfen' + 'sensible daten'.

items.question: Sind sensible Daten oder Metadaten enthalten, bevor ich es weitergebe?
items.note: True positive (de) — 'sensible daten' + 'Metadaten' ('weitergebe' is route-only).

items.question: Are there any confidential information or metadata warnings?
items.note: True positive, KEYWORD-ONLY — 'confidential information' + 'metadata'.

items.question: What changed between these two versions?
items.note: True positive — 'what changed' + 'changed between' (two overlapping phrases → deduped to 2) + *-v1* filename.

items.question: Give me a redline comparing versions of this file.
items.note: True positive — 'redline' word + *-v1*/*-v2* filenames.

items.question: Welche Änderungen gibt es zwischen den beiden Versionen?
items.note: True positive (de) — 'Änderungen' word (not a bare substring) + *entwurf* filename.

items.question: Welche Unterschiede gibt es zwischen den beiden Dokumenten?
items.note: True positive (de) — 'Unterschiede' word + *entwurf* filename.

items.question: Show me a redline of the revision.
items.note: True positive, KEYWORD-ONLY — 'redline' + 'revision'.

items.question: What is the gross amount on this invoice?
items.note: Confusion: an invoice keyword ('invoice','gross amount') while a BANK-named doc is in scope — invoice must win.

items.question: Reconcile the bank statement transactions, please.
items.note: Confusion: a strong BANK keyword ('bank statement','transactions') while an INVOICE doc is in scope — bank must win.

items.question: How much is due on this invoice?
items.note: Confusion: 'invoice' keyword while a *contract* doc is in scope — invoice wins ('how much'/'amount due' are route-only).

items.question: Summarize the key terms of this contract.
items.note: Confusion: 'key terms'/'contract' brief question, no deadline keyword — contract-brief, not deadline-obligation-finder.

items.question: What are the notice periods and cancellation deadlines in this contract?
items.note: Confusion: two deadline keywords outscore the single 'contract' word (both share the *contract* doc signal).

items.question: What changed between these two documents?
items.note: Confusion: contract-named versions in scope, but the question carries NO 'contract' word — only what-changed has a keyword, so contract-brief cannot fire.

items.question: Anonymize the sensitive information before sharing.
items.note: Confusion: 'anonymize' (redaction) vs 'sensitive information' (share-safe) tie at 3 — installId tie-break favours document-redaction, the correct action skill.

items.question: Is it safe to share this file with sensitive information in it?
items.note: Confusion: 'safe to share' + 'sensitive information' (two share-safe keywords) beat any redaction match (there is no redaction verb).

items.question: Summarize the meeting and list the action items.
items.note: Confusion: 'action items' reads deadline-ish, but 'action items' is a MEETING keyword and deadline uses 'action required' — meeting wins.

items.question: What's the weather like today?
items.note: True NEGATIVE — a statement PDF is in scope but the question is unrelated. The lone doc signal must NOT fire (keyword-required).

items.question: Tell me a joke.
items.note: True NEGATIVE — invoice PDF in scope, unrelated question.

items.question: Translate this sentence into French.
items.note: True NEGATIVE — meeting-named doc in scope, unrelated question.

items.question: What is the capital of France?
items.note: True NEGATIVE — bank-named doc in scope, unrelated question.

items.question: How do I cook pasta?
items.note: True NEGATIVE — meeting-named doc in scope, unrelated question.

items.question: What's a good recipe for dinner tonight?
items.note: True NEGATIVE — a *contract* doc in scope, unrelated question (contract/deadline lone doc must not fire).

items.question: Tell me about the history of Rome.
items.note: True NEGATIVE — a *vertrag* doc in scope (contract + deadline both glob it), unrelated question.

items.question: Summarize the key points of this mission statement.
items.note: Near-miss — a filename with 'statement' (a mission statement) and no bank keyword ('statement' is route-only).

items.question: Can you fix the formatting in this file?
items.note: Near-miss — a filename containing 'invoice' with a formatting question, no invoice keyword.

items.question: Rotate this scanned image by 90 degrees.
items.note: Near-miss — a *contract* filename with an image-editing question, no contract keyword.

items.question: I'm trying to find a better work-life balance these days.
items.note: Adversarial — 'balance' is now route-only (not a suggest keyword), so this no longer fires bank.

items.question: Bill called me yesterday about getting lunch.
items.note: Adversarial — 'Bill' the name: 'bill' is route-only now (kept 'billing' as the suggest term), so no invoice fire.

items.question: It only takes a few minutes to walk to the office.
items.note: Adversarial — 'minutes' (time) is route-only now; the offer uses 'meeting minutes'/'meeting' instead.

items.question: Remind me in 10 minutes.
items.note: REPRODUCTION ('in 10 minutes'→Meeting offer): bare 'minutes' is route-only, so no meeting offer fires.

items.question: Wo finde ich die Datenschutzerklärung auf der Website?
items.note: Adversarial (de) — U4/§4.4 DROPPED 'datenschutz' from the redaction vocabulary (the handler acts on neither routeMatch nor PII_TOPIC_RE for it), so it never offers OR auto-fires; it was already word-matched, so 'Datenschutzerklärung' never hit it either.

items.question: Wie kündige ich mein Netflix-Abo?
items.note: REPRODUCTION ('Netflix'→'net'): 'net' is a word-matched ROUTE-only term, so it never hits 'Netflix' and never offers bank.

items.question: Explain the syntax of this tax formula.
items.note: Adversarial — 'tax' ⊂ 'syntax': 'tax' is word-matched route-only, so no invoice offer.

items.question: I assume the sum is roughly right.
items.note: Adversarial — 'sum' ⊂ 'assume': 'sum' is word-matched route-only, so no bank/invoice offer.

items.question: Können wir ein Meeting für Dienstag ansetzen?
items.note: Adversarial — the word-matched 'meeting' offers meeting-protocol on a SCHEDULING ask. The honest precision CEILING of keeping 'meeting' suggest-able (the §4.1 incident requires it); documented, not hidden.

items.question: What time is it?
items.note: Neutral — no trigger word, no doc. Must correctly abstain.

items.question: Sag mir einen Witz.
items.note: Neutral (de) — must correctly abstain.

items.question: Write me a poem about the sea.
items.note: Neutral — must correctly abstain.

items.question: Hello there, how are you?
items.note: Neutral — must correctly abstain.

items.question: Give me three ideas for a birthday gift.
items.note: Neutral with a doc whose MIME alone is a SUPPORTING signal (no filename glob, no keyword) — a lone MIME signal must not clear the bar.

items.question: Give me the cashflow.
items.note: U4/§4.4 — a SINGLE bank keyword ('cashflow') + a statement PDF merely present in the Library (NOT selected). SUGGESTION offers bank (full score 4); AUTO-FIRE narrows the library doc away → keyword-only 2 < 3 → correctly does NOT silently fire. This is the '≥1 doc signal degrades to any matching PDF anywhere' trap the narrowing closes.

items.question: Who is the vendor here?
items.note: U4/§4.4 — a single invoice keyword ('vendor') + a library invoice PDF (not selected). Offer fires (full 4); auto-fire declines (narrowed 2).

items.question: Summarize this meeting.
items.note: U4/§2.4/§4.4 — the '(Summarize this meeting)' incident, but the meeting doc is only IN the library, not attached. Offer fires (full 4); auto-fire declines (narrowed 2) — a matching doc anywhere in the library must not silently apply the skill. Contrast wc-meeting-attached-01.

items.question: Summarize this meeting.
items.note: U4/§2.4 contrast to wc-meeting-weak-01: the SAME question, but the meeting doc is EXPLICITLY in scope (attachment / hand-pick — default 'narrowed'). Auto-fire keeps the doc signal → 'meeting' (2) + mime (1) + filename (1) = 4 ≥ 3 → correctly auto-fires. This is the §2.4 fix working when the user actually put the doc in front of the skill.

items.question: Reconcile the transactions on my bank statement.
items.note: U4/§4.4 — STRONG intent (two distinct bank keywords: 'bank statement' + 'transactions', capped → 4) still auto-fires at whole-corpus scope with NO explicit doc: the narrowing removes only the DOC corroboration, never keyword strength.

items.question: List the line items and the invoice number.
items.note: U4/§4.4 — strong invoice intent ('line items' + 'invoice number', capped → 4) auto-fires at whole-corpus scope without an explicit doc.

items.question: What's a good recipe for dinner tonight?
items.note: U4/§4.4 — an unrelated question with a statement PDF sitting in the Library. Neither suggestion (no keyword) nor auto-fire (no keyword, and the library doc is narrowed away anyway) may fire.

items.question: Was regelt die DSGVO für Unternehmen?
items.note: U4/§4.4 — the audit's exact example: an auto-fired 'Was regelt die DSGVO?' getting a redaction fence. BOTH fixes converge: 'dsgvo' was dropped from the redaction vocabulary (no keyword) AND the library PDF is narrowed away (no doc signal), so redaction neither offers nor auto-fires.

items.question: Wie viel habe ich für Lebensmittel ausgegeben?
items.note: W7/SKA-7 — a German money question whose terms ('wie viel', 'ausgegeben') are ROUTE-ONLY: with the bank skill ACTIVE they route (asserted in skills-vocabulary.test.ts), but they never OFFER, so no suggestion fires even with a Kontoauszug PDF in scope (keyword-required needs a suggest keyword in the question, not a filename).

items.question: Fasse das bitte für mich zusammen.
items.note: W7/SKA-9 — a bare separable imperative ('fasse … zusammen'): route-only (reaches the handler under an active skill; kept on the D56 template by the separable-summary regex), never a suggestion keyword, so it does not offer.

items.question: Thank you very much!
items.note: W7/SKA-11 — a thanks/ack pleasantry (small-talk opt-out in the active-skill router, asserted in skills-vocabulary.test.ts). It carries no suggestion keyword, so it correctly abstains here too.

items.question: What is the most important point of this document?
items.note: W7/SKA-19 — a whole-document SYNTHESIS ask (deliverable-vetoes the needle in the active-skill router). 'important point' is not a suggestion keyword, so no offer fires (routing/veto asserted in skills-vocabulary.test.ts).

items.question: How much did I spend on groceries?
items.note: W7/SKA-20 — the flagship grounded-data question ('how much'/'spend on' are route-only, no offer). The routing decision (grounded-data, not the category template) is asserted in skills-analysis-bank.test.ts.

items.question: Wurde dazu eine Entscheidung dokumentiert?
items.note: W7/SKA-45 — the SINGULAR 'Entscheidung' now offers in its own right (plural 'entscheidungen' existed; the singular was the gap). One keyword + a protocol PDF → meeting-protocol.

items.question: Gab es eine Änderung gegenüber der alten Fassung?
items.note: W7/SKA-45 — the SINGULAR 'Änderung' now offers in its own right (plural 'änderungen' existed). One keyword + a versioned PDF → what-changed.
